mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future earnings and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 6.5 percent, consumer spending growth decelerates sharply and corporate profit margins contract, causing equity valuations to decline over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55 percent of instances where UNRATE crosses above 6.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based aggregate demand growth, which sustains corporate revenue trajectories and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in GDPC1 and RSAFS fail to predict positive 60-day returns (out-of-sample R-squared < 0.01) over any 2-year forward window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the index of industrial production signals immediate supply-side bottlenecks and weakening manufacturing output, triggering short-term risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines are followed by positive 5-day returns in over 60 percent of observations during late-cycle expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the consumer price index crossing above 0.3 percent forces expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which raises discount rates and compresses equity multiples over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL spikes above 0.3 percent MoM are followed by positive 20-day returns in more than 50 percent of cases, or Rank IC remains below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls indicate strengthening labor market conditions and expanding household income, which supports consumer discretionary spending and generates positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no predictive power for 60-day forward returns (t-statistic < 1.5) across a 3-year rolling sample.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between housing starts and retail sales, where housing starts cross below zero while retail sales cross below 0.5 percent MoM, signals a synchronized collapse in durable goods demand, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST crosses below 0 and RSAFS crosses below 0.5% MoM
failure_condition: The joint negative signal fails to predict negative 20-day returns (accuracy < 52 percent) over a 2-year out-of-sample window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in industrial production and nonfarm payrolls during a post-shock recovery phase confirm rapid capacity utilization and labor absorption, driving immediate risk-on flows and positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive co-movements in INDPRO and PAYEMS during recovery phases yield negative 5-day returns in more than 55 percent of instances.
